observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Include in the conduct of actual physical count all land and land improvements owned by the Municipality of Dumalag and all PPE acquired through the 20 percent Development Fund and the Special Education Fund and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) accordingly;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to be consistent in the classification of PPE in their respective records; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to prioritize the reconciliation of PPE in their respective books to establish the accurate balances of various PPE accounts.

2.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Take up the correcting/adjusting entries for the unrecorded accumulated depreciation expense amounting to ₱2.119 million as of December 31, 2024; and
b. Prepare a lapsing schedule of depreciation for all depreciable assets acquired in prior years to facilitate the periodic recording of depreciation of the PPEs.

3.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to include in the IIRUP as of December 31, 2024 the unserviceable properties identified in CY 2023 amounting to ₱6.950 million;
b. Direct the Appraisal Committee to conduct the appraisal activity for all unserviceable properties and submit the corresponding report and the request for disposal to the Audit Team for indorsement to COA Regional Technical Services Office for proper evaluation; and
c. Direct the Disposal Committee to cause the disposal of the unserviceable property soonest upon receipt of the result of the COA-TSO to prevent its further deterioration and to realize income therefrom, if found valuable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Closely monitor the expenses charged vis-à-vis its programmed appropriation and/or supplemental budget, if any; and
b. Programmed Appropriation for the 20% DF shall focus on the nature of investment or capital expenditures and utilization thereof shall be in accordance with Section 3.0 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded under the Calamity Fund so that problems causing a delay in the project implementation would be timely addressed; and

Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate the LDRRMFIP, and as deemed necessary, revise and update to determine the constraints and reasons for failure to fully utilize the 70% LDRRMF and prior year’s unspent/unutilized funds.
We recommended and Management agreed to secure ownership on all properties procured through conveyance of title in the name of government.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the immediate preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record CY 2022 depreciations for PPE to ensure a fair presentation of financial statements pursuant to Section 4(o) of NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers listed in Annex I which are already overdue; and


Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on the timely submission of accounts to facilitate auditorial review.
Secure ownership on all properties procured through conveyance of title in the name of government or securing an indemnity/property bond in case of procurement of untitled lots.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱2.527 million;

Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances granted in prior years; and

Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts by regularly monitoring the outstanding cash advances of officers and employees.

Consolidate all requisitions for materials of similar items quarterly and procure them through public bidding in accordance with the provisions of Sections 10, 48.2, and 52.1 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and Section 26 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

1.11 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all
PPE of the agency pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to exclude unserviceable
properties in the RPCPPE and prepare the corresponding IIRUP,
where these properties should be recorded;
c. Include in the RPCPPE all categories of PPE such as Land, Land
Improvements, Road Network and Systems, Infrastructure, Buildings,
Other Structures, Machineries, Other PPE and the Construction in
Progress accounts; and
d. Direct the Property Officer and the OIC-Accountant to prioritize the
reconciliation of PPE in their respective books to establish the
accurate balances of various PPE accounts.

2.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the OIC-
Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to
derecognize the non-existing properties with a total carrying amount of
₱27,428,630.65 supported with approved Demolition Permit, Request
Letter to COA and the Waste Material Report.

3.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal OIC-Accountant to coordinate with the Property
Officer to trace source documents that can provide necessary
information not available in the accounting office to facilitate
recognition of depreciation expense on all depreciable assets;
b. Instruct the Municipal OIC-Accountant to take up the
correcting/adjusting entries for the unrecorded accumulated
depreciation expense amounting to ₱4,045,767.40 as of December 31,
2023; and
c. Prepare a lapsing schedule of depreciation for all depreciable assets
acquired in prior years to facilitate the periodic recording of
depreciation of the PPEs.

4.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal OIC-Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to take up the correcting/adjusting entries for dropping
the carrying amount of Semi-expendable properties recorded as PPE
amounting to ₱573,446.86; and
b. Instruct the Municipal OIC-Accountant to prioritize the retrieval of
supporting documents to provide complete information in the
Subsidiary Ledgers and require the Property Officer to prepare the
Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued for Semi-expendable
properties to support the derecognition in the books of accounts.

5.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal OIC-Accountant to effect the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts to rectify the erroneous treatment
capitalizing minor repairs, improvement and rehabilitation of PPE
costing ₱591,233.80; and
b. Henceforth, discontinue capitalizing as PPE the costs of minor repairs
and charge the same to surplus/deficit for the prior period transactions
and through the "Repairs and Maintenance Expense" account for the
current year.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 
 
Maximize information dissemination on the availability of the financial assistance to all 2nd district constituents through:

Direct information dissemination with all LGUs and MSWD Officers;

Posting of announcements in the conspicuous places within the barangay; and

Posting of announcement in the newspaper of general circulation within the area.
We recommended and Management agreed to issue the check directly to the biller/client recipient to ensure the validity of payment.

We recommended and Management agreed to completely fill up the Driver’s Trip Ticket and ensure that all signatures contained therein are original to establish validity and authority of the transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Require annual physical inventory to establish the existence, condition, completeness and correctness of its PPE; and

Prepare and submit the corresponding reports on physical count to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. These should be certified correct by the Inventory Committee, approved by the Head LGU or his/her Authorized Representative and reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended and Management agreed to use the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Cards submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as basis for the establishment of RPT/SET Receivables at the start of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Intensify its efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan for 2017-2026 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of preserving the natural resources of the Municipality; and

Convert to controlled dumpsite or develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and eventually, residual wastes of the municipality to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Establish the existence, condition, completeness and correctness of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory; and

Prepare and submit the corresponding reports on physical count to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with the above-cited provisions. These should be certified correct by the Inventory Committee, approved by the Head LGU or his/her Authorized Representative and reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended that Management secure ownership on all properties procured through conveyance of title in the name of government or securing an indemnity/property bond in case of procurement of untitled lots.

We recommended that Management:

Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; and

Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Property Officer to prepare and Issue ARE to persons who were entrusted with government inventories and PPE to ascertain their accountability and responsibility thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Establish the existence, condition, completeness and correctness of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory; and
Prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. These should be certified correct by the Inventory Committee, approved by the Head LGU or his/her Authorized Representative and reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the land owner of the acquired untitled lot for the posting of the indemnity/property bond. Henceforth, secure ownership of properties procured through conveyance of title in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record/update the depreciations for PPE as of CY 2021 to ensure a fair presentation of financial statements pursuant to Section 4(o) of NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱2.527 million;
Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances granted in prior years; and
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts by regularly monitoring the outstanding cash advances of officers and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Draw journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL in order to establish the accurate balances of CIB-LCCA and other related accounts, following the prescribed accounting entry:
a.1 For Unrecorded Deposit:
CIB-LCCA	XXX
Cash Local Treasury/Income/Payable Account	XXX
a.2 For Unrecorded Disbursement/Payroll/Inward Return:
Expense/Asset Account	XXX
CIB-LCCA			XXX
a.3 For Erroneous Entry of Bank Interest:
Income Account	XXX
CIB-LCCA			XXX
a.4 For Stale Check:
a.4.1  Check Cancellation
CIB-LCCA	XXX
Payable Account	XXX
a.4.2  Check Replacement
Payable Account	XXX
CIB-LCCA		XXX
Require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale as required by the above-mentioned Manual; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to evaluate the outstanding checks that were considered stale totaling ₱407,356.77 as of December 31, 2021 for proper treatment in order to undertake the necessary accounting procedures for these stale checks as provided in Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Post all procurements in the PhilGEPS as required by Section 8.3.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Encourage competitive bidding on procurement through the presence of multiple bidders to promote effectiveness and efficiency on the Management of resources.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the official concerned to consolidate requisitions of supplies and materials for the quarter for the issuance of one Purchase Order with a total amount exceeding ₱100,000.00; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to post in the PhilGEPS consolidated requisitions of supplies and materials with amount exceeding ₱100,000.00.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Sufficiently address the availability of the financial assistance to all constituents through:
Direct information dissemination with the local LGUs;
Posting of announcements in the conspicuous places within the barangay; and
Posting with the announcement in the newspaper of general circulation within the area;
Strictly comply with the guidelines on the distribution of financial assistance on all beneficiaries as to the limitations of grant and as to who may avail it; and
Revise and/or improve the guidelines to specify the exact percentage and consistently apply the same guidelines in the distribution of the financial assistance to be granted to individuals seeking the same benefit to promote equality and uniformity of availment.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer install the required billboards/signboards on all PPAs that are being implemented, and to be implemented in the project location in compliance with the above-cited circular to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

In view hereof, we recommended and Management agreed to comply with the foregoing provisions through issuance of ICS/ARE on all properties issued to end-users to identify accountability and enhance security to the items procured.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council to prepare/submit the Supplemental Investment Program approved by the local sanggunian on all Appropriation, Supplemental or Realignments of Budgets included in Annex A in compliance with Section 2.6 of LBC No. 124 and/or Section 2.3 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 01. In addition, submit written valid justification for non-submission and/or non-preparation.

Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted;

Procure directly the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants in accordance with Republic Act 9184 from retailers, dealers or gas stations at retail pump price as provided in GPPB Resolution No. 05-2018 dated May 18, 2018. Immediately stop drawing a cash advance for operating expenses for fuel, oil and lubricants.
observations.managementAction: Due to limited number of suppliers within the Municipality, lack of storage to stock bulk purchase of fuel and emergency utilization of Rescue Teams, payment through checks cannot be practiced at all times.

observations.recommendation:
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.

Inventory Reports

The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Committee has been created, however, due to lack of manpower and voluminous transactions, inventory-taking were not made possible.

Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; and

Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.

Inventory Reports

The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

Submit original copies of Official Receipts for payments under Check No. 0408687 dated 7/23/2018 and Check No. 2268081 dated 7/23/2018.

Submit any Amended Articles of Incorporation duly stamped by SEC, authorizing sale of Heavy Equipment and Machineries as Secondary Purpose of business.

Submit original copies of Delivery Receipts, Charge Invoice, and Official Receipts for payments under Check No. 0408687 dated 07/23/2018 for ₱ 1,807,513.15 and Check No. 2268081 dated 07/23/2018 for ₱ 4,327,344.00.

Submit the needed documents for the evaluation of delivered heavy equipment: Backhoe pursuant to Annexes F & G of COA Circular 2009-011 as follows:

Certificate of Exclusive Distributorship, if applicable

Performance/Quality Test Result (in good condition/operational)

Copy of Brochures/Manuals

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱4.202 million;
Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances granted in prior years; and
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts by regularly monitoring the outstanding cash advances of officers and employees.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management:
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted;
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee
A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.
Property Identification and Coding
Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.
Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.
Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
Direct the Municipal GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration on motor vehicles, dump trucks and heavy equipment in the name of the Municipality of Dumalag on all assets acquired during the year and taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; and
Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that Management identify relevant PPAs and provide corresponding appropriation in the subsequent year’s Annual Budget for LCPC to  strengthen children’s protection and promote overall development of children to their fullest potential.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management:
Submit the required MOA and documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 on Fund Transfers; and
Strictly comply with the Memorandum of Agreement, if any, on the utilization of the fund transferred to the agency.

Fully Implemented

Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilized the 70% LDRRMF; and

Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.
observations.reason:
70% LDRRMF were not fully utilized at the end of the year.
















Partially Implemented

Due to weather conditions, priority PPAs were not all implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management identify relevant PPAs and provide corresponding appropriation in the subsequent year’s Annual Budget for LCPC to  strengthen children’s protection and promote overall development of children to their fullest potential.

We recommended that Management:

Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and

Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person for a constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with Sections 4.8 and 5.2 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW JC No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that Management consolidate all requisitions for materials of similar items quarterly and procure them through public bidding in accordance with the provisions of Sections 10, 48.2, and 52.1 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184  and Section 26 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended that Management:

Submit copy of Sanggunian Resolution endorsing the proposed projects of the LGU

Secure Memorandum of Agreement with the Source Agency, if possible.

Direct the management to come-up with a clear and specific guidelines on the grant of Financial Assistance, taking into consideration:

Limit/ceiling of financial aid to individual (like % on amount of the hospital bill);

Limitations on the frequency of availment (ex: once a year/etc) for a specific period; and

Criteria or priorities as the basis of grant to indigents as far as approval of amount. Especially for medical and scholarship assistance

Come-up with a definite amount for burial assistance for uniformity.

Refrain payment of medical assistance to private hospitals as contemplated in Section 3.4.2.

Prepare quarterly report on fund utilization and status of program/project implementations as provided in Section 6.1.2 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 117 and send written notices to appropriate offices as provided in Section 6.1.4 of same circular.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented






Fully Implemented
















Fully Implemented

Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Submit copies of contract and all supporting documents within five (5) days from the execution of contract as provided in Section B.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Direct the Municipal Engineer to enhance closer monitoring activities on Infra Projects and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Heads to implement immediately funded programs in all on-going/ started projects to attain the program’s objectives; 
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification on low project implementation of BUB Projects in the construction of Water Supply System (1st and 2nd Tranche in San Roque and the Construction of Water Supply System in Sta. Carmen, Sta. Rita, and Sto. Rosario.
Direct the OIC-Accountant to conduct regular  periodic verification and reconciliation of the unexpended balance of fund transfers Due to NGAs Account and related CIP accounts to determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims, and cause the immediate return of any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and
Direct Municipal Agriculturist to submit Program of Work or Work Activities to be undertaken in the DENR fund transfer for the maintenance of National Greening Program, and henceforth, certify in the payroll that each person whose name appears therein have rendered services.
observations.reason:
Submission of copies of contracts were not made on the prescribed period.
















Partially Implemented

Implementation of projects has improved, however, remain incomplete insofar as totality is concerned.
















Partially Implemented

As of Dec. 31, 2019, Phases I and II of San Roque was reported as 100% complete while the Potable Water Supply System Level II, Phase I at Brgy. Sta. Carmen, Sta. Rita, Sto. Rosario was reported as 90% complete.
















Not Implemented

No regular reconciliations were made. 
















Not Implemented

No POW were submitted.
Additional cash advances were still granted despite non-settlement of previous cash advances

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with    Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and 

Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:

Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of two or more employees designated by the head of the agency including the property/administrative officer or custodian depending on the extent of his accountability. 

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. 

Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the corporation concerned. 

These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.

Acknowledgment Receipt for equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.

Inventory Reports

The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the prescribed format by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

Cause the reconciliation of the inventory reports with the accounting and property records.

Direct the Municipal GSO to secure Certificate of Land Title and Certificate of Registration on all real properties and motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality of Dumalag to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.

We recommended that Management consolidate all requisitions for materials of similar items quarterly and procure them through public bidding in accordance with the provisions of Sections 10, 48.2, and 52.1 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184  and Section 26 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended that Management:

Require the BAC to submit the POs with all the supporting documents within five (5) days from date of approval to the Audit Team as provided in Sections 3.1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-01; and

Submit printed copies of Advertisement of Invitation to Bid posted in PhilGEPS on all payments for Infrastructure projects undertaken as provided in Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-01 and 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.reason: Submission was behind the required five days from the execution of contract.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱3,092,394.67;
Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances granted in prior years; and
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts by regularly monitoring the outstanding cash advances of officers and employees.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-03 (2018) dated                February 18, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.”

We reiterate our recommendation that Management:
Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its PPE by conducting the required annual physical inventory and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted;
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee
A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman, the general services officer, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned.
Property Identification and Coding
Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the LGU concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification.
Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.
Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the format prescribed by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.
Direct the Municipal GSO to secure and submit Certificate of Land Title on real properties and Certificate of Registration on motor vehicles and heavy equipment in the name of the Municipality of Dumalag on all assets acquired during the year and taken up in the books to ascertain ownership of properties and propriety of recorded assets.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-02 (2018) dated                February 22, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a valid written justification on the charging made to 20% DF, which could have been a valid expenditure to Capital Outlay of the General Fund; and
Refrain from formulating programs, projects and activities which are not among those enumerated by Section 3.0 DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-009 (2018) dated                February 28, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management submit valid written justification on the realignment/reprogramming made and strictly comply with the provision of Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 on the use of appropriated funds and savings. Otherwise, administrative and/or civil sanctions shall be instituted against the persons held liable.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-004 (2018) dated                February 26, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the LDRRMC to study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP, if needed, revise and update it, and determine or address the constraints/reasons for failure to fully utilized the 70% LDRRMF; and
Effectively, efficiently and economically implement the priority PPAs contained in the approved LDRRMFIP and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) during the current year to attain the objectives pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-008 (2018) dated                February 27, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management identify relevant PPAs and provide corresponding appropriation in the subsequent year’s Annual Budget for LCPC to  strengthen children’s protection and promote overall development of children to their fullest potential.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-005 (2018) dated                February 26, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team for review/validation; and
Henceforth, monitor GAD activities and require the GAD Focal Person for a constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report and submit a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in strict compliance with Sections 4.8 and 5.2 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW JC No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-006 (2018) dated                February 26, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.
Management Comment:
The Management has submitted the CY 2018 GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team on March 12, 2019.

We recommended that Management consolidate all requisitions for materials of similar items quarterly and procure them through public bidding in accordance with the provisions of Sections 10, 48.2, and 52.1 of the Revised 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184  and Section 26 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-010 (2018) dated                February 28, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Submit copy of Sanggunian Resolution endorsing the proposed projects of the LGU
Secure Memorandum of Agreement with the Source Agency, if possible.
Direct the management to come-up with a clear and specific guidelines on the grant of Financial Assistance, taking into consideration:
Limit/ceiling of financial aid to individual (like % on amount of the hospital bill);
Limitations on the frequency of availment (ex: once a year/etc) for a specific period; and
Criteria or priorities as the basis of grant to indigents as far as approval of amount. Especially for medical and scholarship assistance
Come-up with a definite amount for burial assistance for uniformity.
Refrain payment of medical assistance to private hospitals as contemplated in Section 3.4.2.
Prepare quarterly report on fund utilization and status of program/project implementations as provided in Section 6.1.2 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 117 and send written notices to appropriate offices as provided in Section 6.1.4 of same circular.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2018-001-300 (2018) dated                February 19, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.
Management Comment:
Management alleged that the Project Menu is for the medical assistance of individuals either out-patient or confined in the hospital. Moreover, the documentary requirements do not specify that the recipient hospital be of Government establishment only.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management:
Submit copies of contract and all supporting documents within five (5) days from the execution of contract as provided in Section B.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Direct the Municipal Engineer to enhance closer monitoring activities on Infra Projects and direct the Focal Person and Project Proponent Department Heads to implement immediately funded programs in all on-going/ started projects to attain the program’s objectives;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification on low project implementation of BUB Projects in the construction of Water Supply System (1st and 2nd Tranche in San Roque and the Construction of Water Supply System in Sta. Carmen, Sta. Rita, and Sto. Rosario.
Direct the OIC-Accountant to conduct regular  periodic verification and reconciliation of the unexpended balance of fund transfers Due to NGAs Account and related CIP accounts to determine if there are concerned creditors with valid claims, and cause the immediate return of any unexpended amount of fund transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and
Direct Municipal Agriculturist to submit Program of Work or Work Activities to be undertaken in the DENR fund transfer for the maintenance of National Greening Program, and henceforth, certify in the payroll that each person whose name appears therein have rendered services.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-011-100 (2018) dated                April 12, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to:
Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; and
Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2019-007 (2018) dated                February 26, 2019 was issued for the foregoing audit observation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Submit original copies of Official Receipts for payments under Check No. 0408687 dated 7/23/2018 and Check No. 2268081 dated 7/23/2018.
Submit any Amended Articles of Incorporation duly stamped by SEC, authorizing sale of Heavy Equipment and Machineries as Secondary Purpose of business.
Submit original copies of Delivery Receipts, Charge Invoice, and Official Receipts for payments under Check No. 0408687 dated 07/23/2018 for ₱ 1,807,513.15 and Check No. 2268081 dated 07/23/2018 for ₱ 4,327,344.00.
Submit the needed documents for the evaluation of delivered heavy equipment: Backhoe pursuant to Annexes F & G of COA Circular 2009-011 as follows:
Certificate of Exclusive Distributorship, if applicable
Performance/Quality Test Result (in good condition/operational)
Copy of Brochures/Manuals
Submit other needed documents, to wit:
Result of Test Analysis
Such other documents like:
Certificate of Registration from Land Transformation Office
Warranty Certificate
Submit original Corporate Secretary’s Certificate authorizing Mr. Joseph Apsay to sign, execute, and received documents.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the collectors to remit immediately the unreported issued receipts to the Municipal Treasurer and present copies of RCD’s if remitted, and if warranted request the Local Chief Executive to withhold the payment of any money due to collectors to be applied in satisfaction of the indebtedness.
Direct Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day or when deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00;
Direct all collectors/Municipal Treasurer to prepare RCD with complete details and for the Municipal Treasurer to record immediately collections and deposits in the cashbook to establish the correct cash in vault balances at any point in time;
Direct all collectors to prepare and submit monthly RAAF to report the quantity of accountable forms received, issued or cancelled as of the end of the month. 
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and to consolidate the RAAF of accountable officers to be submitted to the unit auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month, and
Use the RIS every time accountable forms are requested/issued to various collectors to establish accountability of each collector to whom accountable forms are issued.
observations.reason:
Some collectors failed to remit collections to the Municipal Treasurer on time.

Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management refrain from charging expenditures against 30% QRF without Local Sanggunian Resolution and a declaration of state of calamity as required under Section 5.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated           September 12, 2012.

We also recommended for the replenishment of the fund in the event that the declaration was not made.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in conformity with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the SWM Board:

Strengthen the implementation of the 10-Year SWM Plan and to incorporate in the CY’s AIP the following PPAs, to wit:

a.1 Collection

a.1.1Intensify SWM Collection activities to assess compliance of solid  waste collection routes with schedules and targeted areas;

a.1.2 Provision of necessary training to collectors and  personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste; and

a.1.3 Provide the needed facilities to improve the delivery of waste collection service

a.2 Disposal

Plan for the safe closure of dumpsite and/or rehabilitates the controlled dump site indicating the time frame and financial requirements.

Direct the Solid Waste Management Board to Create a monitoring and evaluation committee to assess the implementation effectiveness and efficiency of collection and disposal of solid waste and  in all components of SWM.

Direct the SWM Board to closely coordinate with the Planning Officer and Budget Officer to provide specific budgetary allocations for all PPAs identified in the SWM Plan for an effective program implementation particularly, such as, but not limited on the following:

c.1 Solid waste collection, specifically for the following:

Improvement of facilities like acquisition of Garbage Truck and compactor:
Fuel, Gasoline and Oil under SWM PPAs
Repairs and Maintenance of transportation equipment under SWM PPAs
Other General Services

We recommended the following:

Stop the practice of procuring supplies, materials and meals and snacks through reimbursement;

Strictly adhere to the provision of  Section 172 (b) of GAAM, Volume 1 and paragraphs 2 and 3 of Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated  March 20, 2007 in instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check; and

Follow the provision of the Revised Implementing Rules and regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) 9184 and Section 93 of PD 1445 in the procurement of supplies, materials, meals and snacks.

We recommended that Management:

Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with    Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
 
Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures:
Creation of Inventory Committee

A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of two or more employees designated by the head of the agency. 

Property Identification and Coding

Government property shall be properly labeled and identified by the Inventory Committee

Acknowledgment Receipt for equipment (ARE)

An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. 

Inventory Reports
The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the prescribed format by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 


II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

Cause the reconciliation of the inventory reports with the accounting and property records.
observations.reason: Not implemented. Since the physical inventory report is not prepared at year end, the accounting would have the difficulty in the reconciliation with accounting records.

Stop the practice of procuring supplies, materials and meals and snacks through reimbursement;
Strictly adhere to the provision of  Section 172 (b) of GAAM, Volume 1 and paragraphs 2 and 3 of Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated  March 20, 2007 in instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check; and
Follow the provision of the Revised Implementing Rules and regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) 9184 and Section 93 of PD 1445 in the procurement of supplies, materials, meals and snacks.

We recommended that Management

Require the concerned municipal officials to strengthen and hasten the full implementation of PPAs programmed in the CY’s 20% AIP, either by administration or contract, if necessary; 

Review unimplemented PPAs in CY 2016 and ascertain viability of implementation for the ensuing year, otherwise, reprogram for a more viable and significant PPAs in accordance to the socio-economic development and environmental management requirements.

Refrain from using the 20% DF for expenditures not related to the implementation of development programs and projects or items that are expressly indicated not to be paid out of the 20% DF; and

Henceforth, strictly comply with Item 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, Sections 455 b.2 and 109 (5) of               RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to record fund transfer following the prescribed accounting entry:

Due from NGA 			     XXX.XX
			Cash In Bank 			XXX.XX

and make the necessary adjustments to properly recognize fund transfers following the adjusting entry as shown;

Due from NGA		XXX.XX
		Prior Year’s Adjustment/		XXX.XX
		Other MOOE
	
Require the accountable officer to submit report on the implementation and utilization of the funds received from the Municipal government;

We recommended that Management:

Consolidate all requisitions for supplies and equipment of similar items quarterly and procure them through public bidding;  and

b. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 10 and 48.2 of the Revised 2016 IRR of             R.A. No. 9184, and Section 26 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended that Management cause the reconciliation of the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2016 with the Property Ledger Cards maintained by the Municipal Accountant and General Ledger Balances  of  PPEs for CY 2016 totaling ₱145.644 million.
